name: understand-chat
tagline: Answers codebase questions by grepping a pre-built knowledge-graph JSON and checking its freshness against git before answering.
verdict: Clean, read-only skill: no network calls, no secrets touched, no destructive commands, and it defensively guards its own git-diff invocation against argument injection. Only minor doc gaps (no explicit non-triggers, no worked example, no version field) keep it from a perfect score.

highlights.summary: No explicit 'do not use when...' guidance in the description or body (e.g. when the graph is absent or wildly stale beyond the auto-warning).
No worked example of a query and its expected subgraph/answer is given, which could leave ambiguous cases (e.g. multi-match keywords) under-specified.
No version field or changelog/change-signal anywhere in the frontmatter or body.
